name: Caroline Better Together — Food Pantries Flyer

lines: Blessing Boxes are small outdoor pantries where neighbors can help each other by providing free anonymous access tonon-perishable food and other essential items to anyone in need. Take if You Need, Give If You Can
Food Boxes only by appointment as needed.

paragraphs: FOOD PANTRIES & RESOURCESIN CAROLINE COUNTY CAROLINE BETTER TOGETHER BLESSING BOX LOCATIONS Blessing Boxes are small outdoor pantries where neighbors can help each other by providing free anonymous access tonon-perishable food and other essential items to anyone in need. Take if You Need, Give If You Can Martin's House and Barn 14374 Benedictine Lane Tues, Thurs, Fri 8:30 - 11:30 am Wednesday, 6 - 7:30 pm 410-634-2537 Ridgely United Methodist Church 109 Central Avenue Monthly on 2nd & 4th Wed., 2 - 4 pm Spirit and Mind Incorporated Food Boxes only by appointment as needed. 302-331-1010 Ridgely Preston Aaron’s Place Mobile at Preston Lions Club 114 Williamson Street 4th Wed, 10 am February, April, June, August, October, December LNT Seafood 233 Main Street Everyday, 9 am - 5 pm 410-829-9393 Denton Aaron’s Place 100 N. 6 Street t h Every Tuesday, 11:30 am to 4 pm Every Wednesday, 9:30 am to 3 pm 3rd Thursday, 11 am January, March, May, July, September, November Soup Kitchen Tuesday, 11:00 am 410-479-3298 Samaritan House 12 N. 5th Street Wednesday, Thursday, Friday 10 am - 2 pm Saturday 9 am - Noon Call for Required Information 410-479-1251 Thinking of You Pantry Living Waters Assembly 9058 Double Hills Road Every Wednesday, 7 pm If you are in need of immediatate food assistance please reach out directly to these organizations. Many provide emergency food boxes as well as scheduled pantry events.Need Immediate Food Assistance? Marydel Aarons Place Mobile at Iglesia de Dios El Buen Camino 18161 Henderson Road 4th Thursdays, 11 am, February, April, June, August, October, December Goldsboro Giving Grace Goldsboro Fire Hall, 700 Old Line Road 2nd Saturday of each month, 7 am — until food is gone Sponsored by NCCA 410-829-3158 Federalsburg Aaron’s Place at Community Civic League 3439 Laurel Grove Road 1st and 3rd Wednesday of Every Month 4 pm to 6 pm Mobile Pantry 4th Thursday, 11 am January, March, May, July, September, November 410-479-0292 Aaron’s Place at Union Methodist Church 301 N Main Street Sundays after service, 11:30 am to 1 pm 410-754-9210 Aaron’s Place - Federalsburg Elementary Judy Center Early Learning Hub 323 S. University Avenue Monday - Thursday, 9 am-3 pm Following CCPS calendar 410-479-3050 Greensboro Aaron’s Place in Greensboro 111 S. Main Street Monthly, 1st and 3rd Thursday, 10 am - 1 pm 410-479-0292 St Paul United Methodist Church 300 Sunset Avenue Saturdays, 10 am - Noon Food For Learning Caroline County Public Schools Program Info & Request Form 410-479-3261 Weekend Backpack Program SCAN HERE FOR MORE RESOURCES Look for more community resources andupcoming events by scanning the QR Code orvisiting carolinebettertogether.org R e s o u r c e L is t C o m p ile d b y C a r o lin e H u m a n S e r v ic e s C o u n c il. c a r o lin e h s c .o r g C o r d o v a Talbot County Cordova Firehouse 11864 Kittys Corner Rd. Federalsburg Park Lane Church of God – Parking Lot Morris Ave. between Main and Park Ridgely UMC 107 Central Ave. New Beginnings GMC 12020 Central Ave.Ridgely St. Luke's GMC 100 S. Fifth Ave. Behind the church His Hope Office 105 Gay St. Thawley's Chapel 10030 Reed Rd. Denton Goldsboro T r i n i t y U M C 302 Church Ln. Marydel Community Hall 130 Halltown Rd. Greater Impact Church 13699 Greensboro Rd. Near the Community Park Entrance 217 E Sunset Ave. Greensboro Preston Bethesda GMC 155 Main St. Lions Club Community Center 114 Williamson St. Bethlehem Wesleyan Church 5330 Bethlehem Rd. locations_meta.disclaimer: Columns are inferred from lines before each standalone phone number. PDF text order may not match the printed multi-column flyer. Sites without a phone line on this page may be missing; verify against the PDF.
